title: W3 Ignite Model Test - JavaScript Specialty

questions.text: What is the output of `console.log(1 + '2' + 3)`?
questions.Explanation: JavaScript performs string concatenation from left to right. 1 + '2' becomes '12', then '12' + 3 becomes '123'.

questions.text: Which keyword is used to declare a block-scoped variable that cannot be reassigned?
What is the result of `[] == ![]`?
questions.Explanation: This is a quirk of JS coercion. ![] is false. [] == false performs type conversion resulting in 0 == 0, which is true.

questions.text: Which method is used to add an element to the beginning of an array?
What is the purpose of the 'use strict' directive?
options: To make the code run faster
To enforce stricter parsing and error handling
To prevent the use of comments

questions.text: What is the output of `typeof []`?
questions.Explanation: In JavaScript, arrays are technically specialized objects.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a falsy value in JavaScript?
questions.Explanation: An empty array [] is truthy. Only 0, '', null, undefined, NaN, and false are falsy.

questions.text: What does the `map()` method return?
options: A new array with the results of a function on every element

questions.text: How do you check if a property 'x' exists in an object 'obj'?
What is a 'Closure' in JavaScript?
options: A function that has access to its outer function's scope
A way to end a loop

questions.text: Which operator is used to check both value and type equality?
What is the output of `console.log(Boolean('false'))`?
questions.Explanation: Any non-empty string is 'truthy' in JavaScript, even the string 'false'.

questions.text: Which built-in method sorts the elements of an array?
What is 'Hoisting'?
options: Moving declarations to the top of the scope
Increasing the speed of the script
Lifting an element in the DOM

questions.text: What is the result of `null == undefined`?
questions.Explanation: Using loose equality (==), null and undefined are equal to each other.

questions.text: Which statement is used to handle exceptions?
What does the 'Temporal Dead Zone' refer to?
options: The time between a variable declaration and its initialization with let/const
The time it takes for a script to load
A period where the CPU is idle

questions.text: Which function is used to parse a string into an integer?
What is the output of `3 > 2 > 1`?
questions.Explanation: 3 > 2 is true. Then true > 1 is evaluated. true is coerced to 1, and 1 > 1 is false.

questions.text: Which method merges two or more arrays?
How do you create an arrow function?
What is the value of `this` inside an arrow function?
options: It is lexically inherited from the parent scope

questions.text: Which API is used for making asynchronous HTTP requests in modern JS?
What is the output of `console.log(typeof NaN)`?
Which method removes the last element from an array?
What does `JSON.stringify()` do?
options: Converts a JavaScript object into a JSON string

questions.text: In the DOM, how do you select an element with id 'app'?
What is the result of `'5' - - '2'`?
questions.Explanation: The minus operator coerces both strings to numbers. 5 - (-2) = 7.

questions.text: Which event occurs when the user clicks on an HTML element?
What is the purpose of `Promise.all()`?
options: To wait for all given promises to resolve or any to reject

questions.text: What is the output of `0 == '0'`?
How do you write a comment in JavaScript?
Which keyword is used to wait for a Promise inside an async function?
What is the output of `!!'hello'`?
questions.Explanation: The first ! converts the string to boolean false, the second ! flips it to true.

questions.text: Which method returns the length of a string?
questions.Explanation: .length is a property, not a method.

questions.text: Which operator returns the remainder of a division?
What is 'Event Bubbling'?
options: Events moving from the root to the target
Events moving from the target element up to the root

questions.text: How do you declare a constant in JavaScript?
What is the result of `typeof null`?
Which function is used to execute a function after a specific number of milliseconds?
What is the Spread Operator?
What is the output of `console.log(2 + 2 + '2')`?
Which method converts a JSON string into a JavaScript object?
How can you prevent a form from submitting when a button is clicked?
What does the `find()` method do on an array?
options: Returns all elements that match a condition
Returns the value of the first element that passes a test
Returns the index of an element

questions.text: What is a 'Pure Function'?
options: A function that only uses local variables
A function that always returns the same output for the same input and has no side effects
A function that is written in pure JS

questions.text: Which scope does a variable declared with `var` have?
What is the output of `console.log(1 < 2 < 3)`?
questions.Explanation: 1 < 2 is true. Then true < 3 is evaluated. 1 < 3 is true.

questions.text: Which method removes the first element from an array?
What is the default value of a variable that has been declared but not assigned?
